campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 19, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council held a meeting on May 19, 2026, where they discussed staffing vacancies and efforts to recruit and retain employees. The council also addressed public input on various matters and noted that the Boogie Festival was happening that weekend.
keyTopics: City staffing vacancies and recruitment/retention strategies
Public hearing on government code section 3502 matters
Boogie Festival weekend event

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — May 6, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council approved an engineer's report for the upcoming fiscal year and scheduled votes on a new ordinance for May 20th. The council also discussed whether Saratoga should become a charter city and reviewed potential tax options that could generate additional revenue.
keyTopics: Engineer's Report approval for fiscal year planning
New ordinance scheduled for May 20th vote
Charter city status evaluation
Potential tax options and revenue estimates

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — June 2, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met on June 2, 2026, to approve meeting minutes, confirm a new Youth Commissioner, and set up the November 2026 municipal election for three open seats. The council also approved development plans, authorized the Town Manager to sign several service agreements, and reviewed the budget.
keyTopics: November 3, 2026 general municipal election scheduled for three Town Council seats
New Youth Commissioner confirmed by selection committee
Tract 10675 Boulevard development project final map approved
Multi-year software agreement with Accela, Inc. for town operations
Budget authorization and expenditure approvals

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 2, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met on June 2, 2026, to approve routine meeting minutes and handle administrative matters. The council also reviewed budget reports and assessment plans for two business improvement districts—Alum Rock Santa Clara Street and The Alameda—for the upcoming fiscal year, and scheduled public hearings to discuss the proposed assessments.
keyTopics: Alum Rock Santa Clara Street Business Improvement District FY 2026-2027 budget and assessments
The Alameda Business Improvement District FY 2026-2027 budget and assessments
Public hearings scheduled for assessment levy decisions
Routine approval of prior meeting minutes

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 2, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on June 2, 2026 with both in-person and virtual attendance options. Residents could participate remotely via Zoom video conference or phone dial-in to watch proceedings and provide public comment.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access available via Zoom and phone dial-in
Public comment procedures for remote participants (raise hand in Zoom or press *9 on phone)
In-person and hybrid meeting format for council proceedings

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — June 2, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special meeting on June 2, 2026, with a closed session followed by a public special order meeting. The specific agenda items and decisions from these sessions were not detailed in the publicly available information.
keyTopics: Closed session held at 5:30 PM
Special order of the day items addressed at 6:30 PM

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — June 1, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met on June 1, 2026, to review and approve meeting minutes from their April 6 Legislative Review Committee session. The council also received a legislative update and began discussing a new policy proposal, though the agenda document appears incomplete.
keyTopics: April 6, 2026 Legislative Review Committee minutes approval
Public comment period (3-minute limit per speaker)
Legislative update briefing
New policy consideration (details cut off in agenda)

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — June 1, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's City Council is holding meetings for a Stadium Neighborhood Relations Ad Hoc Subcommittee to discuss issues affecting the neighborhood around the stadium. Residents can attend in person or join online via Zoom, and can submit written comments by email before each meeting.
keyTopics: Stadium Neighborhood Relations Ad Hoc Subcommittee meetings
In-person and remote participation options available
Written public comment submission process (email by 10 a.m. before meeting)

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve routine administrative items and consider changes to how the city uses federal housing and community development funds. The council also reviewed requests from individual council members for new work items and discussed a potential change to the rules around filling council vacancies.
keyTopics: Updates to federal housing and community development funding plan for 2025-2026
Proposed change to council vacancy ordinance
New work requests submitted by council members
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming meeting calendar

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 6, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a full-day meeting covering planning and development projects, public safety operations (police, fire, and emergency services), proposed fee changes for the next fiscal year, and infrastructure improvements. Council reviewed staffing, service priorities, and capital spending plans across multiple city departments.
keyTopics: Planning and Development Services updates (projects O:337-352)
Police, Fire, and Emergency Services presentations and operations
FY 2027 Municipal Fee Schedule proposed increases
Capital Improvement Program and infrastructure projects
Enterprise and Internal Service Fund projects
